@graph.name: SynFraud-1M
@graph.summary: Synthetic dataset of one million financial transactions for fraud detection model training and benchmarking.
@graph.description: A fully synthetic dataset simulating one million credit card transactions generated using a Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) conditioned on statistical properties of real transaction data. Designed to overcome privacy barriers preventing sharing of real financial transaction data. Contains transactions labeled as legitimate (97%) or fraudulent (3%), with 22 features per transaction including synthetic merchant category, transaction amount, geographic coordinates, and time of day. No real customer data is present.
@graph.dataset_dataCollectionProcess: No real data collected. Transactions generated by a TabGAN model trained on a confidential reference dataset of 500,000 real transactions (held internally; not distributed). The generative model learned statistical distributions and correlations of transaction features. After generation, a separate privacy audit confirmed no record-level similarity between synthetic outputs and real training records (nearest-neighbor distance ratio > 0.7).
dataset_dataPreprocessing: Post-generation filtering: transactions with amounts exceeding 5 standard deviations from the per-merchant-category mean removed
Geographic coordinates snapped to valid country grids
Class balance fixed at 97:3 legitimate:fraudulent by oversampling fraudulent class from generator outputs
Feature scaling: amount log-normalized; latitude/longitude normalized to [-1,1]
@graph.dataset_datasetNoise: Synthetic generation introduces distributional noise relative to real transaction patterns. The GAN model may not capture rare but meaningful fraud patterns that were underrepresented in the reference dataset. Researchers should validate model performance on real transaction data before production deployment.
dataset_knownBias: Fraud patterns reflect attack vectors observed in the reference dataset (2019-2022); emerging fraud techniques not represented
Merchant category distribution reflects one regional market; international transaction patterns may differ
@graph.dataset_intendedUse: Training and benchmarking of fraud detection classifiers in research settings. Specifically designed for researchers who lack access to real transaction data. Not a substitute for validation on real-world data before deployment.
@graph.dataset_datasetUpdateMechanism: Static release. Updates will be published as new major versions if the reference statistical model is retrained.
@graph.comment: SPDX 3.0 NOTE: This example demonstrates 'dataset_datasetType: [structured]' for GAN-generated synthetic tabular data and 'dataset_datasetNoise' to document known limitations of synthetic generation. Note: 'syntactic' in SPDX refers to data about grammatical/syntactic structure (e.g. parse trees), NOT artificially generated data - do not use 'syntactic' for synthetic datasets. Also: 'dataset_datasetSize' (314572800 bytes) and 'dataset_intendedUse' are DEPRECATED in SPDX 3.1 - use 'software_artifactSize' (same unit: bytes) and Core 'intendedUse' instead.

Added as a workaround for the lack of https://spdx.org/licenses/CC0-1.0 as a valid ListedLicense in SPDX 3.0.1 RDF. This will be removed once https://github.com/spdx/LicenseListPublisher/issues/183 is implemented.
